mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is continuous and does not require a discrete trigger.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following declines are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by at least 0.5 percentage points from its trailing 12-month low (Sahm-rule-style trigger), the labor market signals an onset of recession risk that compresses forward equity risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.5pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.1% as of 2024-09 vintage, near cycle lows but trending up
failure_condition: In >55% of historical trigger instances since 2005, 60d forward returns are positive, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional 60d mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of FEDFUNDS staying elevated while CPIAUCSL growth decelerates implies a rising real policy rate, which tightens financial conditions and depresses S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. Neither series alone captures the real-rate tightening effect; both must move together.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5% while 6-month annualized CPIAUCSL growth decelerates by > 1pp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=5.13% and CPIAUCSL=314.686 as of Sep 2024 vintage
failure_condition: The interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is not significantly greater than the sum of individual FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL effects, tested via nested regression F-test (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST (housing starts) while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% indicates rate-sensitive demand destruction specific to a monetary tightening regime, foreshadowing weaker consumer and investment spending and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days. In easing or low-rate regimes the same HOUST decline does not carry the same signal.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised to 1391k units Aug-2024, FEDFUNDS at 5.13%
failure_condition: Outside of tightening regimes, or during tightening regimes overall, triggered HOUST declines fail to produce below-median 60d forward returns in > 55% of instances, rejecting the regime-dependence claim.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, which is priced into equities with a short lag, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This is a continuous always-on trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 100.4309 vs vintage 103.1389 Aug-2024, showing large revision risk
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 5d forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign accuracy is not statistically distinguishable from 50% (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS (retail sales) year-over-year growth crosses above 4%, it signals robust consumer demand strength that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Below this threshold the signal is inactive.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised to 697039M Aug-2024 from vintage 710773M
failure_condition: Following threshold crossings, 20d forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances since 2005, or mean triggered return is not significantly above unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with a simultaneous uptick in UNRATE signals labor force re-entry rather than genuine labor market weakness, a nuance the market misprices in the short run, producing a transient positive S&P 500 reaction over the next 5 trading days. Neither PAYEMS nor UNRATE alone identifies this reclassification effect.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth > 150k while UNRATE rises simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised to 157912k from vintage 159105k, UNRATE=4.1% Sep-2024
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction signal fails to outperform a PAYEMS-only model on out-of-sample Rank IC in more than half of rolling 2-year windows since 2010.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 4% year-over-year while the Fed maintains FEDFUNDS above 5% (a restrictive monetary regime), sustained tight policy erodes equity valuations through prolonged multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. In regimes where inflation is below 3%, this mechanism does not apply.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=5.13% Sep-2024 while CPIAUCSL YoY has been decelerating toward 2-3% range
regime_hint: restrictive monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: During periods meeting the joint threshold, 60d forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).
